_note: Golden labels for the meeting-notes fuzzy demo. Three targets at increasing fuzziness. Each item is a claim the judge evaluates PASS/FAIL against the transcript; labels are what human expert(s) said. The THIRD target deliberately carries TWO experts' labels that disagree — because 'infer the unspoken real need' has no reliable ground truth, and that low human-human agreement is the honest result, not a bug.

targets.name: Extract explicitly stated requirements
targets.fuzziness: low — this is really CHECKABLE_WITH_REFERENCE: was X said out loud?
targets.criterion: Is this requirement EXPLICITLY stated in the transcript?

items.claim: Base compensation up to 210k
Go or Rust as the primary language
Comfortable with Kafka or similar event streaming
Seven-plus years of experience mentioned
Fintech/payments background is required
Candidate must relocate to NYC full-time
Strong Postgres / database experience
A signing bonus was offered

targets.name: Judge the manager's ATTITUDE / emphasis (tone)
targets.fuzziness: medium — reading emphasis and hesitation, not facts
targets.criterion: Does the manager clearly signal this as a real priority (by tone/emphasis), not just mention it?

items.claim: Collaboration / working-style is a top priority
The manager is flexible on the '2 days in office' policy
The manager is anxious about team morale
The manager is in a rush to fill the seat at any cost
Raw technical brilliance is the single most important trait
The manager cares more about scale experience than years

targets.name: Infer the UNSPOKEN real need (read between the lines)
targets.fuzziness: high — inferring intent never said aloud; even experts disagree
targets.criterion: Is this the candidate's/manager's TRUE underlying need, even if not stated directly?
targets.note: Two human experts labeled this independently. Their disagreement is the point: there is no reliable ground truth for 'what he really means', so κ will be low and the gate will honestly BLOCK it.

items.claim: The role is effectively fully-remote-friendly despite the 'hybrid' label
'Senior' really means 'emotionally stabilizing', not just technically senior
The manager would actually accept a mid-level engineer with great EQ
Payments-domain experience secretly matters more than he admits
The 'end of quarter' timeline is soft and he'll wait months for the right person
He is really looking for a future tech-lead, not an individual contributor
Compensation can stretch well past the stated 220k for the right person
